---
layout: default
title: "National Political and Legislative Brief: July 2, 2026"
date: 2026-07-02T11:03:45.054034+00:00
summary: "Parliament debates the Union Budget amid high-stakes legislative moves, while the Supreme Court addresses AI-generated legal risks and state election mandates"
run_time_ist: "4:33PM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 4:33PM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Parliamentary proceedings:</strong> The Lok Sabha is currently debating the Union Budget, with Speaker Om Birla recusing himself from proceedings until the pending no-confidence motion is resolved.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I3</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Legislative and electoral updates:</strong> A controversial bill proposing to disqualify arrested Chief Ministers and Prime Ministers is set for a parliamentary return, while the Congress party has officially announced its six candidates for the upcoming Rajya Sabha biennial elections.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimgFBVV95cUxNTE1ySWFxVEVyS0dCcTNzWkhNbFI2RlFEV1pXRWk0MTJRLXpRbWExMWotMkNmNkx4WEVNUkoxdEdiYWg0cTBFSHpfcWhtaUt1a0FvMG5XTjN0N3pYcDdjanpHQXdfVnhmV1ptYTJaTlZ5V1NWVjNlWWlZYk5KZ1NXM2YwUlVJVlVvZm5tcGNPeWZIMGlhR01IbkxB?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I8</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I9</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Election oversight:</strong> The Election Commission of India and State Election Commissioners concluded a national round table conference in New Delhi to discuss electoral integrity and administrative coordination.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitwFBVV95cUxNMDdrUnZqWDZVODhIYllhWkx6QmQxbHFQN1g1Z1ZhMlNhWkEtQ2FRUU9yWU5QZ25iYlJSaWd1MWtELXdSVk8zcTlXdjc2M1RfckJqSEotMHFDSElQTjM4eVVfZ0Fod09WU3ptRE92LV9MQ2IyU1VMa19mTm1iN1hsZ0xPelhRcHhJdXpER3RKRUNkQ2NSTVhMV2hFTUtQWmFabUJWTGhEemludnVvV2ppQUc1UFZvRm8?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I10</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Judicial integrity:</strong> The Supreme Court has mandated a new AI policy following the discovery of fake AI-generated judgments being used in insolvency litigation, declaring a &quot;zero tolerance&quot; stance on the practice.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W12</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>State governance and legal challenges:</strong> The Supreme Court upheld the extension of Manipur’s panchayat polls until October, while the Meghalaya government has petitioned the apex court to challenge the bail granted to the accused in the high-profile Sonam Raghuvanshi murder case.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W9</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W5</a></span></li>
</ul>
